title: storage-v3 #4: v2 → v3 auto-migrator on Storage::open
body:
# Goal

Auto-migrate a v2-shape repo (data on the `issues` bookmark) to
a v3-shape repo (per-issue refs under `refs/jjf/issues/<id>`,
per-key memory refs under `refs/jjf/memories/<key>`,
`refs/jjf/meta/format-version` sentinel) on first `Storage::open`.

End state: opening a v2 repo with v3 jjforge auto-migrates it
once, then it's a v3 repo forever. Every v2 op becomes one v3
commit on the right per-issue ref, preserving the full op-log
audit trail. The `issues` bookmark is deleted after migration.

# Why now

Ticket 4 of 8 in the v3 storage epic (`bd98097`). Depends on
tickets 1, 2, and 3 — needs v3 write helpers, v3 read helpers,
and the init sentinel. After this ticket lands, jjforge stops
needing the v2 dance entirely on existing repos.

# Approach

Mirrors the shape of the v1 → v2 migrator
(`Storage::maybe_migrate_v1_to_v2`,
`crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs:892–1000`).

**Detection.** At `Storage::open`:

- `refs/jjf/meta/format-version` exists → v3, done.
- `refs/jjf/meta/format-version` absent AND `issues` bookmark
  exists → v2, run the migrator.
- `refs/jjf/meta/format-version` absent AND `bugs` bookmark
  exists → v1, run the existing v1 → v2 migrator first, then
  fall through into v2 → v3.
- Neither exists → fresh repo, nothing to migrate.

**Migration steps (per the design doc):**

1. List every issue id from the v2 `issues` bookmark by
   enumerating `issues/<id>.json` files at the bookmark tip
   (`jj file list -r bookmarks(issues)` or equivalent; the
   v1 → v2 walker shows the pattern).

2. For each issue:
   a. Walk the v2 op log for that issue via
      `history::read_history_at` — get the full chain of
      commits whose trailer's `Jjf-Issue:` (or v1 `Jjf-Bug:`)
      matches `<id>`, in oldest-first order.
   b. For each op-commit in that chain, build a v3 commit:
      - Parent: previous v3 commit on `refs/jjf/issues/<id>`,
        or none for the very first.
      - Tree: contains `issue.json` (and `comments.jsonl` if
        present) AT THAT POINT IN HISTORY — i.e. replay the
        ops up to and including this one to derive the
        snapshot, then write that snapshot as the blob.
      - Message: the v2 commit's message, verbatim (summary +
        trailer block). The trailer block is unchanged.
   c. `git update-ref refs/jjf/issues/<id> <last-v3-commit>`
      to plant the ref.

3. Same loop for memories: enumerate `memories/<key>.json`,
   walk the per-key op log, replay onto
   `refs/jjf/memories/<key>`.

4. Delete the `issues` bookmark
   (`git update-ref -d refs/heads/issues` works; jj
   bookmarks are git refs). The working copy is not touched.

5. Write `refs/jjf/meta/format-version` per ticket 3's shape.
   This is the idempotency marker — subsequent opens see it
   and skip the migrator.

**Preserves op-log granularity.** Every v2 op-commit becomes
one v3 op-commit on the per-issue ref. `git log
refs/jjf/issues/<id>` post-migration shows the same audit
trail as the v2 bookmark log filtered to that issue.

**Atomicity.** The migration is NOT atomic across all issues
— if it crashes halfway through, some refs are populated and
some aren't, and the sentinel isn't written yet. Recovery: the
next `Storage::open` re-detects v2 (no sentinel) and runs the
migrator again. The migrator must be idempotent per-issue: if
`refs/jjf/issues/<id>` already exists, skip that issue (or
verify and skip; depends on caution level).

# Scope

In scope:
- New `Storage::maybe_migrate_v2_to_v3` function in
  `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs`.
- Wire it into `Storage::open` alongside the existing v1 → v2
  migrator (v1 → v2 runs first if needed; v2 → v3 runs second).
- Per-issue replay of the op log onto the new ref.
- Delete `issues` bookmark on success.
- Write the format-version sentinel last.

Out of scope:
- Reverse migration (v3 → v2). One-way only.
- Concurrent-migration safety. Single-writer assumption is
  fine; if two processes open the repo concurrently and both
  try to migrate, that's a race we accept (`git update-ref`
  CAS would catch most of it but not all). The design doc
  explicitly says "one-shot and irreversible."
- A `jjf migrate` CLI verb. Auto-migration on open is enough;
  if we want an explicit verb later, file a new ticket.

# Acceptance

- [ ] A v2 repo with 5+ issues (including some closed, some
      with comments, some with multi-op commits) migrates to
      v3 on first open.
- [ ] Every issue's v3 ref-chain has the same number of
      op-commits as the v2 bookmark log filtered to that
      issue.
- [ ] `read_record(id)` returns the same `Record` pre- and
      post-migration.
- [ ] `read_comments(id)` returns the same comments pre- and
      post-migration.
- [ ] The `issues` bookmark is absent post-migration.
- [ ] `refs/jjf/meta/format-version` exists post-migration.
- [ ] Re-opening a migrated repo is a no-op (no second
      migration runs).
- [ ] v1 → v2 → v3 chained migration works on a v1 repo (the
      34 archived `refs/bugs/*` refs in this repo are an
      adversarial test bed if needed, though they're in the
      git-bug format not the jjforge-v1 format — so probably
      a synthetic v1 repo is the right test).
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` green.

# Pointers

- Design doc: `docs/storage-out-of-tree.md` ("v2 → v3
  migration" section).
- Reference: `Storage::maybe_migrate_v1_to_v2`
  (`crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs:892–1000`) — same shape,
  same wiring into `Storage::open`.
- Test: `v1_to_v2_migration_preserves_history` integration
  test — model the v2 → v3 test on it.

# Out-of-scope reminder

Do NOT modify the v2 op-commit chain. The migrator READS v2
data and WRITES v3 data; the v2 bookmark stays until the
last step (the bookmark delete). If the migrator crashes,
v2 is still readable.
